title: carum graveolens
intro: Carum graveolens, also known as caraway, is a biennial herb native to Europe and Asia, belonging to the parsley family (Apiaceae). The plant has been valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its seeds, which contain carvone, a compound with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. Carum graveolens is used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, nausea, and respiratory issues, and is also believed to have anti-anxiety and antibacterial properties. The seeds of the caraway plant are used in herbal preparations such as tea, oil, and capsules, and are also used as a spice in cooking.
